title: PM Estimator VBA Lineage Scope-Sheet Intake
objective: Use the historical estimator-to-Dataverse VBA modules as static mapping evidence to add read-only scope-sheet estimator intake support for the larger Project Miner Building A/B workbook, while preserving the existing flat Updated/Quote Tab reader and no-mutation PM lane posture.
active_role: Delegated Technical Repo Authority / PM Runtime Product Coordinator
dependencies: C:/Users/jjswe/Desktop/Project Miner PM Planning/Cupertino - Miner Estimator PHX Bldg A & B MV Rev9.xlsm
C:/Users/jjswe/Desktop/Project Miner PM Planning/Building B IFC.pdf
C:/Users/jjswe/Desktop/Project Miner PM Planning/Estimator R3 - Project Miner Temp Power Testing.xlsm
C:/Users/jjswe/Desktop/Project Miner PM Planning/Miner Temp SLD-AP-BCARRASCO.pdf
orchestration_inputs: The user recalled two historical VBA modules created to let the estimator serve as the project intake source.
Coordinator review found the modules targeted Dataverse-era clipboard/file JSON and mapping verification workflows, but preserved the relevant estimator workbook map.
Current Project Miner workbooks do not include Dataverse_Import, but both include Equipment Reference.
The smaller Miner Temp workbook remains flat-reader compatible through Updated.
The larger Building A/B estimator is scope-sheet based and needs Equipment Reference driven discovery before it can be used as a PM intake source.
constrained_outputs: The flat estimator reader still prefers Updated and then Quote Tab when those sheets contain line items.
A scope-sheet reader now discovers active estimator scopes from Equipment Reference column L/M rows with nonzero totals.
Scope-sheet metadata includes source_format, scope_sheets, scope_count, scope_sheet, scope_type, scope_total_hours, scope_multiplier, scope_quoted_amount, and source_row context.
Scope-sheet apparatus rows use quantity from column C, equipment type from column E, hours per unit from column I, and line total from column J.
Quantity expansion still produces one apparatus candidate per unit.
The preview script now accepts explicit estimator workbook, SLD PDF, equipment workbook, and capability workbook override arguments.
The PM intake runbook now records the two historical VBA modules as static lineage evidence and forbids macro execution during read-only intake.
